_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize GA batch: 3 senators (dickerson SD21, jones SD22, burns SD23) + 2 representatives (ridley HD22, fincher HD23). State tier. Evidence-first; null where no cited evidence; every TRUE/FALSE cited.

profile.background: Co-founder of Quest Trucking; heads Purple Chip Capital Mgmt. Lifelong Cherokee County resident. Sworn in Oct 14, 2025 after a 2025 special-election runoff (61%) succeeding Brandon Beach (now US Treasurer). SD-21 covers parts of Cherokee Co. and North Fulton (Canton). Committees: Banking & Financial Institutions (Sec.), Public Safety, Natural Resources, Veterans/Military/Homeland Security.

jason-dickerson@GA.notes_set: Brand-new senator (Oct 2025), thin documented record. Scored only on clear campaign-stated positions (pro-life; deport illegal immigrants); all other cells null for lack of cited evidence. Sponsored SB 513 (chronic-absenteeism 'Every Day Counts Act', passed Senate Feb 2026, failed House) and SB 429 (disabled-veteran housing) — neither maps to a rubric cell. No third-party conservative scorecard rating (GRTL/NRA/Frontline) located. Confirmed on 2026 GOP primary ballot (May 19, 2026).

0.note: Campaign agenda 'Uphold the right to life'; pledged to 'fight to protect life' (SOFT campaign self-statement).

1.note: Campaign agenda explicitly 'Deport illegal immigrants' (SOFT campaign self-statement).

profile.background: Senate Democratic (Minority) Leader, elected Jan 2025. In office since Jan 2015, SD-22 (Augusta/Richmond Co.). Former prosecutor — first African American elected Solicitor General of Richmond County (2004, re-elected 2008). BA South Carolina State; JD North Carolina Central. Committees: Judiciary, Public Safety, Reapportionment & Redistricting, State Institutions & Property, Higher Education.

harold-v-jones-ii@GA.notes_set: Mainstream Democratic Senate leader. Despite a prosecutorial background, his documented criminal-justice record runs reform/defense-oriented, NOT a conservative law-and-order deviation: opposed SB 92 (DA/prosecutor oversight commission, 2023) calling it a 'bureaucratic nightmare', and voted AGAINST SB 63 (cash-bail expansion, 2/23/2023). Sponsored SB 202 (2026) reciprocal misdemeanor discovery (defendant-protective). No conservative cross-party deviations found. Individual roll calls on abortion (HB 481), marriage, vouchers (SB 233), guns (SB 319), and immigration (HB 1105) were not retrievable from cited sources, so those cells are null (not inferred from party). Confirmed running 2026.

1.note: Documented contrary: opposed SB 92 (DA/prosecutor oversight commission) and voted AGAINST SB 63 (cash-bail expansion, 2/23/2023) — i.e., against the cashless-bail/rogue-prosecutor accountability position the rubric scores as aligned.

profile.background: State senator SD-23 (Sylvania/Screven, east GA) since Jan 2021; Chairman, Senate Committee on Higher Education (2025). US Army Reserves veteran. Georgia Tech BS, Georgia State MBA & PhD; professor of information systems at Georgia Southern; former president of Gordon State College; former US Rep GA-12 (2003-05). Sponsored SB 222 (Zuckerbucks ban, signed 2023).

max-burns@GA.notes_set: Documented conservative; scored from iVoterGuide/Vote Smart candidate survey (self-statements), signature legislation, and an NRA PVF endorsement. Sponsored SB 222 (private-election-funding 'Zuckerbucks' ban, signed 2023). Praised HB 1105 immigration enforcement ('standing firmly against illegal immigration'). No cross-party deviations found. Several individual roll calls (SB 140, SB 233, SB 63, SB 319) not separately confirmable (LegiScan blocked), so those cells are null rather than inferred from the party-line outcome. iVoterGuide overall: 'Conservative (Conditional).' Confirmed on 2026 GOP primary ballot.

0.note: Candidate survey: opposes abortion in all circumstances ('None'); supports defunding abortion providers and the Born Alive Survivors Protection Act.
Survey: 'strongly agrees' government should define marriage as one man / one woman.

4.note: Survey: opposes adding sexual-orientation/gender-identity to non-discrimination law.

1.note: Survey: government 'should not discriminate against' those holding the traditional-marriage belief — conscience protection.

4.note: Sponsored SB 222, the Georgia ban on private ('Zuckerbucks') election funding, signed into law 2023 (HARD signature legislation).

0.note: Survey: supports a border wall and 'only legal immigrants.'

2.note: Praised HB 1105 (bans sanctuary policies, immigration enforcement) — 'standing firmly against illegal immigration'; survey: no funding for sanctuary cities.

0.note: NRA PVF endorsed; survey supports 'no restrictions' gun ownership and teachers carrying — aligns with permitless/constitutional carry.

1.note: NRA PVF endorsement + survey 'no restrictions' = opposition to red-flag/magazine/AWB/registry gun control (HARD endorsement).

profile.background: State representative HD-22 (Cherokee/Cobb, Woodstock) since Jan 2023; reelected Nov 2024 (60.5%). BS Public Policy, Georgia State (2019); owns Southern Horticultural Management. Vice Chair Code Revision; Secretary Energy/Utilities & Telecom. Turning Point Action lifetime key-issues score 100; Freedom Index 55%.

jordan-ridley@GA.notes_set: Consistent conservative voting record (TPAction 86.96 live 2026 / 100 lifetime; Border 100%, Taxes 100%, Educational Freedom 91.67%). HARD votes: YES on SB 140 (gender-transition ban for minors) and the trans-sports act; YES to prohibit foreign nationals in campaigns; YES on SB 442 (revoke migrant licenses) and SB 420 (foreign-owned-land restrictions); YES to make obstruction of law enforcement a felony. iVoterGuide survey: photo ID required to vote; businesses need not provide services violating religious beliefs; opposes assisted suicide; 'None' gun restrictions needed. One notable CROSS-PARTY DEVIATION: one of ~8 House Republicans to vote AGAINST Gov. Kemp's tort-reform package (does not map to a rubric cell — noted only). SB 233 and HB 1105 individual roll calls not separately confirmable (LegiScan blocked) → those cells null.

0.note: Campaign: 'I stand firm on my commitment to the sanctity of life,' protect life 'from the first heartbeat to the last.'

3.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Disagree' with legalizing assisted suicide/euthanasia.

2.note: Voted YES on SB 140 (ban on gender-transition procedures for minors) and YES on the Fair & Safe Athletic Opportunities (trans-sports) Act; iVoterGuide disagrees with adding gender identity as a protected class.

4.note: Voted YES on trans-sports act; iVoterGuide opposes gender identity as a protected class — opposes LGBTQ promotion in public policy/schools.

0.note: TPAction Educational Freedom 91.67%; campaign supports 'continued expansion of school choice' and 'parents ultimate authority over education.'

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Disagree' that businesses must provide services violating their religious beliefs — conscience exemption.
iVoterGuide: 'Strongly Disagree' with allowing voting without photo ID — supports photo voter ID.

4.note: Voted YES to prohibit foreign nationals in campaigns — opposes foreign election interference.

0.note: Voted YES to make obstruction of law enforcement a felony — affirmative pro-law-enforcement vote.
iVoterGuide 'Strongly Agree' US should secure the southern border; TPAction Border Security 100%.

4.note: Voted YES on SB 420 (restrictions on foreign-owned farmland).

0.note: Campaign: right to keep and bear arms 'unquestionable freedom'; iVoterGuide: 'None' gun restrictions needed — aligns with constitutional carry.

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'None' restrictions needed; 'Strongly Disagree' victims should sue dealers/manufacturers — opposes red-flag/registry/AWB-style gun control.

profile.background: State representative HD-23 (Canton/Cherokee Co.). Won a Jan 6, 2026 special-election runoff (~70%+) and sworn in Jan 10, 2026, filling the seat left vacant by Rep. Mandi Ballinger's death (Oct 2025). 35+ yr career: probation/parole officer, FBI Special Agent (Chicago), prosecutor/Assistant DA (Atlanta & Appalachian circuits). Mercer University; Woodrow Wilson College of Law. Owns Plum Nelly RV Park.

bill-fincher@GA.notes_set: Brand-new member (sworn Jan 2026), zero voting record / no sponsored bills found. Scored only on clear campaign-stated positions (protect pro-life policies; support school choice; 'back the blue'). Strong economy/property-tax platform did not map to a rubric cell (cells are CBDC/balanced-budget/usury/ESG). Areas with no citable position (marriage, religious liberty, election, federalism, immigration, specific 2A) are null. No third-party endorsements/ratings (GRTL/NRA/Frontline) located. Career FBI/prosecutor background supports the pro-law-enforcement statement.

0.note: Campaign pledge to 'protect pro-life policies' (clear self-stated pro-life position).
Campaign supports School Choice 'allowing parental selection of learning environments.'
Campaign pledge to 'back the blue and strengthen public safety'; career FBI Special Agent and prosecutor (affirmative pro-law-enforcement).
